expectations.text: The draft identifies the request as a feature ticket and uses the feature template structure with Description and Acceptance criteria.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states: 'Used feature template with Description and Acceptance criteria in GIVEN/WHEN/THEN form.'

expectations.text: The acceptance criteria are checklist items written in GIVEN / WHEN / THEN form.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states: 'Acceptance criteria in GIVEN/WHEN/THEN form.'

expectations.text: The response references notifications flow details such as NotificationsScreen, NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsViewDataMapper, selectedSites, or hasUnreadNotifications.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states references to NotificationsScreen, NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsViewDataMapper, selectedSites, hasUnreadNotifications.

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states 'Stopped at draft/approval mode.' No issue was created (no body file or issue URL found).

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: conversation.md states 'Title not duplicated in body.'

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: No body file persists for verification (issue was not created, draft mode). No evidence of code fence violation.

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations passed. The subagent followed the skill workflow correctly: used the feature template, wrote GIVEN/WHEN/THEN acceptance criteria, referenced relevant codebase components, stopped at draft/approval mode, and did not duplicate the title. No body file was created since the issue was not submitted.
